L. 115–52, § 303(b)(2)(C)“(B) Twenty-four percent shall be derived from fees under subsection (a)(3) (relating to abbreviated new drug applications and supplements). L. 112–193, § 2(c)126 Stat. 1443Oct. 5, 2012“(1)Notwithstanding section 744B(a)(2)(E)(ii) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (), the fee authorized under section 744B(a)(2) of such Act for fiscal year 2013 shall be due 30 calendar days after publication of the notice provided for in section 744B(a)(2)(C)(i) of such Act.21 U.S.C. 379j–42(a)(2)(E)(ii)“(2)Notwithstanding section 744B(a)(3)(C)(ii) of such Act, the fee authorized under section 744B(a)(3) of such Act for fiscal year 2013 shall be due on the later of—“(A)the date of submission of the abbreviated new drug application or prior approval supplement for which such fee applies; or“(B)30 calendar days after publication of the notice referred to in section 744B(a)(3)(B)(i) of such Act.“(3)Notwithstanding section 744B(a)(4)(D)(i) of such Act, the fee authorized under section 744B(a)(4) of such Act for fiscal year 2013 shall be due not later than 45 days after the publication of the notice under section 744B(a)(4)(C)(i) of such Act.”

Fees Authorized for Fiscal Year 2013, , , provided that:Pub. L. 112–193, § 2(c)126 Stat. 1443Oct. 5, 2012“(1)Notwithstanding section 744B(a)(2)(E)(ii) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (), the fee authorized under section 744B(a)(2) of such Act for fiscal year 2013 shall be due 30 calendar days after publication of the notice provided for in section 744B(a)(2)(C)(i) of such Act.21 U.S.C. 379j–42(a)(2)(E)(ii)“(2)Notwithstanding section 744B(a)(3)(C)(ii) of such Act, the fee authorized under section 744B(a)(3) of such Act for fiscal year 2013 shall be due on the later of—“(A)the date of submission of the abbreviated new drug application or prior approval supplement for which such fee applies; or“(B)30 calendar days after publication of the notice referred to in section 744B(a)(3)(B)(i) of such Act.“(3)Notwithstanding section 744B(a)(4)(D)(i) of such Act, the fee authorized under section 744B(a)(4) of such Act for fiscal year 2013 shall be due not later than 45 days after the publication of the notice under section 744B(a)(4)(C)(i) of such Act.”

children.heading: One-time backlog fee for abbreviated new drug applications pending onOctober 1, 2012
children.label: 1 One-time backlog fee for abbreviated new drug applications pending onOctober 1, 2012

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Each person that owns an abbreviated new drug application that is pending on , and that has not received a tentative approval prior to that date, shall be subject to a fee for each such application, as calculated under subparagraph (B).October 1, 2012

children.heading: Method of fee amount calculation
children.label: B Method of fee amount calculation
children.text: The amount of each one-time backlog fee shall be calculated by dividing $50,000,000 by the total number of abbreviated new drug applications pending on , that have not received a tentative approval as of that date.October 1, 2012

children.heading: Notice
children.label: C Notice
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall publish in the Federal Register a notice announcing the amount of the fee required by subparagraph (A).October 31, 2012

children.heading: Fee due date
children.label: D Fee due date
children.text: The fee required by subparagraph (A) shall be due no later than 30 calendar days after the date of the publication of the notice specified in subparagraph (C).

children.heading: Sunset
children.label: E Sunset
children.text: This paragraph shall cease to be effective .October 1, 2022

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Each person that owns a Type II active pharmaceutical ingredient drug master file that is referenced on or after , in a generic drug submission by any initial letter of authorization shall be subject to a drug master file fee.October 1, 2012

children.heading: One-time payment
children.label: B One-time payment
children.text: If a person has paid a drug master file fee for a Type II active pharmaceutical ingredient drug master file, the person shall not be required to pay a subsequent drug master file fee when that Type II active pharmaceutical ingredient drug master file is subsequently referenced in generic drug submissions.

children.heading: Notice
children.label: C Notice
children.text: Not later than 60 days before the start of each of fiscal years 2023 through 2027, the Secretary shall publish in the Federal Register the amount of the drug master file fee established by this paragraph for such fiscal year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to subsection (g)(2)(C), for a generic drug submission to reference a Type II active pharmaceutical ingredient drug master file, the drug master file must be deemed available for reference by the Secretary.

children.label: I
children.text: the person that owns a Type II active pharmaceutical ingredient drug master file has paid the fee required under subparagraph (A) within 20 calendar days after the applicable due date under subparagraph (E); and

children.label: II
children.text: the drug master file has not failed an initial completeness assessment by the Secretary, in accordance with criteria to be published by the Secretary.

children.heading: List
children.label: iii List
children.text: The Secretary shall make publicly available on the Internet Web site of the Food and Drug Administration a list of the drug master file numbers that correspond to drug master files that have successfully undergone an initial completeness assessment, in accordance with criteria to be published by the Secretary, and are available for reference.

children.label: I
children.text: the date on which the first generic drug submission is submitted that references the associated Type II active pharmaceutical ingredient drug master file; or

children.label: II
children.text: the date on which the drug master file holder requests the initial completeness assessment.

children.label: I
children.text: 30 calendar days after publication of the notice provided for in subparagraph (C); or

children.label: II
children.text: 30 calendar days after the date of enactment of an appropriations Act providing for the collection and obligation of fees for such year under this section.

children.heading: Abbreviated new drug application filing fee
children.label: 3 Abbreviated new drug application filing fee

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Each applicant that submits, on or after , an abbreviated new drug application shall be subject to a fee for each such submission in the amount established under subsection (d).October 1, 2012

children.heading: Notice
children.label: B Notice
children.text: Not later than 60 days before the start of each of fiscal years 2023 through 2027, the Secretary shall publish in the Federal Register the amount of the fees under subparagraph (A) for such fiscal year.

children.heading: Fee due date
children.label: C Fee due date
children.text: The fees required by subparagraphs (A) and (F) shall be due no later than the date of submission of the abbreviated new drug application or prior approval supplement for which such fee applies.

children.heading: Refund of fee if abbreviated new drug application is not considered to have been received, is withdrawn prior to being received, or is no longer received
children.label: D Refund of fee if abbreviated new drug application is not considered to have been received, is withdrawn prior to being received, or is no longer received

children.heading: Applications not considered to have been received and applications withdrawn prior to being received
children.label: i Applications not considered to have been received and applications withdrawn prior to being received
children.text: The Secretary shall refund 75 percent of the fee paid under subparagraph (A) for any abbreviated new drug application that the Secretary considers not to have been received within the meaning of for a cause other than failure to pay fees, or that has been withdrawn prior to being received within the meaning of .section 355(j)(5)(A) of this titlesection 355(j)(5)(A) of this title

children.heading: Applications no longer received
children.label: ii Applications no longer received
children.text: The Secretary shall refund 100 percent of the fee paid under subparagraph (A) for any abbreviated new drug application if the Secretary initially receives the application under and subsequently determines that an exclusivity period for a listed drug should have prevented the Secretary from receiving such application, such that the abbreviated new drug application is no longer received within the meaning of .section 355(j)(5)(A) of this titlesection 355(j)(5)(A) of this title

children.heading: Fee for an application the Secretary considers not to have been received, or that has been withdrawn
children.label: E Fee for an application the Secretary considers not to have been received, or that has been withdrawn
children.text: An abbreviated new drug application that was submitted on or after , and that the Secretary considers not to have been received, or that has been withdrawn, shall, upon resubmission of the application or a subsequent new submission following the applicant’s withdrawal of the application, be subject to a full fee under subparagraph (A).October 1, 2012

children.heading: Additional fee for active pharmaceutical ingredient information not included by reference to Type II active pharmaceutical ingredient drug master file
children.label: F Additional fee for active pharmaceutical ingredient information not included by reference to Type II active pharmaceutical ingredient drug master file
i
children.text: such submission contains information concerning the manufacture of an active pharmaceutical ingredient at a facility by means other than reference by a letter of authorization to a Type II active pharmaceutical drug master file; and

children.label: ii
children.text: a fee in the amount equal to the drug master file fee established in paragraph (2) has not been previously paid with respect to such information.

children.heading: Generic drug facility fee and active pharmaceutical ingredient facility fee
children.label: 4 Generic drug facility fee and active pharmaceutical ingredient facility fee

children.heading: Generic drug facility
children.label: i Generic drug facility
children.text: Each person that owns a facility which is identified in at least one generic drug submission that is approved to produce one or more finished dosage forms of a human generic drug shall be assessed an annual fee for each such facility.

children.heading: Active pharmaceutical ingredient facility
children.label: ii Active pharmaceutical ingredient facility
children.text: Each person that owns a facility which is identified in at least one generic drug submission in which the facility is approved to produce one or more active pharmaceutical ingredients or in a Type II active pharmaceutical ingredient drug master file referenced in at least one such generic drug submission, shall be assessed an annual fee for each such facility.

children.heading: Facilities producing both active pharmaceutical ingredients and finished dosage forms
children.label: iii Facilities producing both active pharmaceutical ingredients and finished dosage forms
children.text: Each person that owns a facility identified in at least one generic drug submission that is approved to produce both one or more finished dosage forms subject to clause (i) and one or more active pharmaceutical ingredients subject to clause (ii) shall be subject only to the fee attributable to the manufacture of the finished dosage forms for that facility.

children.heading: Amount
children.label: B Amount
children.text: The amount of fees established under subparagraph (A) shall be established under subsection (d).

children.heading: Notice
children.label: C Notice
children.text: Within the timeframe specified in subsection (d)(1), the Secretary shall publish in the Federal Register the amount of the fees under subparagraph (A) for such fiscal year.

children.label: i
children.text: the first business day on or after October 1 of each such year; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the first business day after the enactment of an appropriations Act providing for the collection and obligation of fees for such year under this section for such year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A generic drug applicant program fee shall be assessed annually as described in subsection (b)(2)(E).

children.heading: Amount
children.label: B Amount
children.text: The amount of fees established under subparagraph (A) shall be established under subsection (d).

children.heading: Notice
children.label: C Notice
children.text: Within the timeframe specified in subsection (d)(1), the Secretary shall publish in the Federal Register the amount of the fees under subparagraph (A) for such fiscal year.

children.label: i
children.text: the first business day on or after October 1 of each such fiscal year; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the first business day after the date of enactment of an appropriations Act providing for the collection and obligation of fees for such fiscal year under this section for such fiscal year.

children.label: A
children.text: if it is submitted via a Food and Drug Administration electronic gateway, on the day when transmission to that electronic gateway is completed, except that a submission or master file that arrives on a weekend, Federal holiday, or day when the Food and Drug Administration office that will review that submission is not otherwise open for business shall be deemed to be submitted on the next day when that office is open for business; or

children.label: B
children.text: if it is submitted in physical media form, on the day it arrives at the appropriate designated document room of the Food and Drug Administration.

children.heading: Fiscal year 2023
children.label: A Fiscal year 2023
children.text: For fiscal year 2023, fees under subsection (a) shall be established to generate a total estimated revenue amount under such subsection of $582,500,000.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: For each of the fiscal years 2024 through 2027, fees under paragraphs (2) through (5) of subsection (a) shall be established to generate a total estimated revenue amount under such subsection that is equal to the base revenue amount for the fiscal year under clause (ii), as adjusted pursuant to subsection (c).

children.heading: Base revenue amount
children.label: ii Base revenue amount
children.text: The base revenue amount for a fiscal year referred to in clause (i) is equal to the total revenue amount established under this paragraph for the previous fiscal year, not including any adjustments made for such previous fiscal year under subsection (c)(3).

children.label: A
children.text: Five percent shall be derived from fees under subsection (a)(2) (relating to drug master files).

children.label: B
children.text: Thirty-three percent shall be derived from fees under subsection (a)(3) (relating to abbreviated new drug applications).

children.label: C
children.text: Twenty percent shall be derived from fees under subsection (a)(4)(A)(i) (relating to generic drug facilities). The amount of the fee for a contract manufacturing organization facility shall be equal to twenty-four percent of the fee for a facility that is not a contract manufacturing organization facility. The amount of the fee for a facility located outside the United States and its territories and possessions shall be $15,000 higher than the amount of the fee for a facility located in the United States and its territories and possessions.

children.label: D
children.text: Six percent shall be derived from fees under subsection (a)(4)(A)(ii) (relating to active pharmaceutical ingredient facilities). The amount of the fee for a facility located outside the United States and its territories and possessions shall be $15,000 higher than the amount of the fee for a facility located in the United States, including its territories and possessions.

children.label: I
children.text: If a person (including its affiliates) owns at least one but not more than 5 approved abbreviated new drug applications on the due date for the fee under this subsection, the person (including its affiliates) shall be assessed a small business generic drug applicant program fee equal to one-tenth of the large size operation generic drug applicant program fee.

children.label: II
children.text: If a person (including its affiliates) owns at least 6 but not more than 19 approved abbreviated new drug applications on the due date for the fee under this subsection, the person (including its affiliates) shall be assessed a medium size operation generic drug applicant program fee equal to two-fifths of the large size operation generic drug applicant program fee.

children.label: III
children.text: If a person (including its affiliates) owns 20 or more approved abbreviated new drug applications on the due date for the fee under this subsection, the person (including its affiliates) shall be assessed a large size operation generic drug applicant program fee.

children.label: ii
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, an abbreviated new drug application shall be deemed not to be approved if the applicant has submitted a written request for withdrawal of approval of such abbreviated new drug application by April 1 of the previous fiscal year.

children.label: A
children.text: one;

children.label: B
children.text: the average annual percent change in the cost, per full-time equivalent position of the Food and Drug Administration, of all personnel compensation and benefits paid with respect to such positions for the first 3 years of the preceding 4 fiscal years multiplied by the proportion of personnel compensation and benefits costs to total costs of human generic drug activities for the first 3 years of the preceding 4 fiscal years; and

children.label: C
children.text: the average annual percent change that occurred in the Consumer Price Index for urban consumers (Washington-Arlington-Alexandria, DC–VA–MD–WV; Not Seasonally Adjusted; All items; Annual Index) for the first 3 years of the preceding 4 years of available data multiplied by the proportion of all costs other than personnel compensation and benefits costs to total costs of human generic drug activities for the first 3 years of the preceding 4 fiscal years.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Beginning with fiscal year 2024, the Secretary shall, in addition to the adjustment under paragraph (1), further increase the fee revenue and fees under this section for a fiscal year, in accordance with this paragraph, to reflect changes in the resource capacity needs of the Secretary for human generic drug activities.

children.label: i
children.text: be derived from the methodology and recommendations made in the report titled “Independent Evaluation of the GDUFA Resource Capacity Planning Adjustment Methodology: Evaluation and Recommendations” announced in the Federal Register on (85 Fed. Reg. 46658); andAugust 3, 2020

children.label: ii
children.text: incorporate approaches and attributes determined appropriate by the Secretary, including approaches and attributes made in such report, except that in incorporating such approaches and attributes the workload categories used in forecasting resources shall only be the workload categories specified in section VIII.B.2.e. of the letters described in section 3001(b) of the Generic Drug User Fee Amendments of 2022.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Under no circumstances shall an adjustment under this paragraph result in fee revenue for a fiscal year that is less than the sum of the amounts under subsection (b)(1)(B)(ii) (the base revenue amount for the fiscal year) and paragraph (1) (the dollar amount of the inflation adjustment for the fiscal year).

children.label: aa
children.text: the total number of abbreviated new drug applications submitted was greater than or equal to 2,000; or

children.label: bb
children.text: thirty-five percent or more of abbreviated new drug applications submitted related to complex products (as that term is defined in section XI of the letters described in section 3001(b) of the Generic Drug User Fee Amendments of 2022);

children.label: aa
children.text: the total number of abbreviated new drug applications submitted was greater than or equal to 2,300; or

children.label: bb
children.text: thirty-five percent or more of abbreviated new drug applications submitted related to complex products (as so defined);

children.label: aa
children.text: the total number of abbreviated new drug applications submitted was greater than or equal to 2,300; or

children.label: bb
children.text: thirty-five percent or more of abbreviated new drug applications submitted related to complex products (as so defined); and

children.label: aa
children.text: the total number of abbreviated new drug applications submitted was greater than or equal to 2,300; or

children.label: bb
children.text: thirty-five percent or more of abbreviated new drug applications submitted related to complex products (as so defined).

children.heading: Publication in Federal Register
children.label: D Publication in Federal Register
children.text: The Secretary shall publish in the Federal Register notice referred to in subsection (a) the fee revenue and fees resulting from the adjustment and the methodology under this paragraph.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For fiscal year 2024 and each subsequent fiscal year, the Secretary may, in addition to adjustments under paragraphs (1) and (2), further increase the fee revenue and fees under this section for such fiscal year if such an adjustment is necessary to provide operating reserves of carryover user fees for human generic drug activities for not more than the number of weeks specified in subparagraph (B) with respect to that fiscal year.

children.label: i
children.text: 8 weeks for fiscal year 2024;

children.label: ii
children.text: 9 weeks for fiscal year 2025; and

children.label: iii
children.text: 10 weeks for each of fiscal year 2026 and 2027.

children.heading: Decrease
children.label: C Decrease
children.text: If the Secretary has carryover balances for human generic drug activities in excess of 12 weeks of the operating reserves referred to in subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall decrease the fee revenue and fees referred to in such subparagraph to provide for not more than 12 weeks of such operating reserves.

children.heading: Rationale for adjustment
children.label: D Rationale for adjustment
children.text: If an adjustment under this paragraph is made, the rationale for the amount of the increase or decrease (as applicable) in fee revenue and fees shall be contained in the annual Federal Register notice under subsection (a) publishing the fee revenue and fees for the fiscal year involved.

children.heading: Fiscal years 2023 through 2027
children.label: 1 Fiscal years 2023 through 2027
children.text: Not later than 60 days before the first day of each of fiscal years 2023 through 2027, the Secretary shall establish the fees described in paragraphs (2) through (5) of subsection (a), based on the revenue amounts established under subsection (b) and the adjustments provided under subsection (c).

children.heading: Fee for active pharmaceutical ingredient information not included by reference to Type II active pharmaceutical ingredient drug master file
children.label: 2 Fee for active pharmaceutical ingredient information not included by reference to Type II active pharmaceutical ingredient drug master file
i
children.text: the total number of such active pharmaceutical ingredients in such submission; and

children.label: ii
children.text: for each such ingredient that is manufactured at more than one such facility, the total number of such additional facilities; and

children.label: B
children.text: the amount equal to the drug master file fee established in subsection (a)(2) for such submission.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The total amount of fees charged, as adjusted under subsection (c), for a fiscal year may not exceed the total costs for such fiscal year for the resources allocated for human generic drug activities.

children.heading: Leasing and necessary equipment
children.label: 2 Leasing and necessary equipment
children.text: Beginning on , the authorities under shall include only leasing and necessary scientific equipment.October 1, 2023section 379j–41(12)(C) of this title

children.heading: Required submission of facility identification
children.label: 1 Required submission of facility identification
children.text: Each person that owns a facility described in subsection (a)(4)(A) or a site or organization required to be identified by paragraph (3) shall submit to the Secretary the information required under this subsection each year. Such information shall, for each fiscal year, be submitted, updated, or reconfirmed on or before June 1 of the previous fiscal year.

children.label: A
children.text: identification of a facility identified in an approved or pending generic drug submission;

children.label: B
children.text: whether the facility manufactures active pharmaceutical ingredients or finished dosage forms, or both;

children.label: C
children.text: whether or not the facility is located within the United States and its territories and possessions;

children.label: D
children.text: whether the facility manufactures positron emission tomography drugs solely, or in addition to other drugs;

children.label: E
children.text: whether the facility manufactures drugs that are not generic drugs; and

children.label: F
children.text: whether the facility is a contract manufacturing organization facility.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Any person that owns or operates a site or organization described in subparagraph (B) shall submit to the Secretary information concerning the ownership, name, and address of the site or organization.

children.label: i
children.text: a site in which a bioanalytical study is conducted;

children.label: ii
children.text: a clinical research organization;

children.label: iii
children.text: a contract analytical testing site; or

children.label: iv
children.text: a contract repackager site.

children.heading: Notice
children.label: C Notice
children.text: The Secretary may, by notice published in the Federal Register, specify the means and format for submission of the information under subparagraph (A) and may specify, as necessary for purposes of this section, any additional information to be submitted.

children.heading: Inspection authority
children.label: D Inspection authority
children.text: The Secretary’s inspection authority under shall extend to all such sites and organizations.section 374(a)(1) of this title

children.heading: Effect of failure to pay fees
children.label: g Effect of failure to pay fees

children.heading: Generic drug backlog fee
children.label: 1 Generic drug backlog fee
children.text: Failure to pay the fee under subsection (a)(1) shall result in the Secretary placing the person that owns the abbreviated new drug application subject to that fee on a publicly available arrears list, such that no new abbreviated new drug applications or supplement submitted on or after , from that person, or any affiliate of that person, will be received within the meaning of until such outstanding fee is paid. This paragraph shall cease to be effective on .October 1, 2012October 1, 2022section 355(j)(5)(A) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: Failure to pay the fee under subsection (a)(2) within 20 calendar days after the applicable due date under subparagraph (E) of such subsection (as described in subsection (a)(2)(D)(ii)(I)) shall result in the Type II active pharmaceutical ingredient drug master file not being deemed available for reference.

children.label: i
children.text: Any generic drug submission submitted on or after , that references, by a letter of authorization, a Type II active pharmaceutical ingredient drug master file that has not been deemed available for reference shall not be received within the meaning of unless the condition specified in clause (ii) is met.October 1, 2012section 355(j)(5)(A) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: The condition specified in this clause is that the fee established under subsection (a)(2) has been paid within 20 calendar days of the Secretary providing the notification to the sponsor of the abbreviated new drug application or supplement of the failure of the owner of the Type II active pharmaceutical ingredient drug master file to pay the drug master file fee as specified in subparagraph (C).

children.label: i
children.text: If an abbreviated new drug application or supplement to an abbreviated new drug application references a Type II active pharmaceutical ingredient drug master file for which a fee under subsection (a)(2)(A) has not been paid by the applicable date under subsection (a)(2)(E), the Secretary shall notify the sponsor of the abbreviated new drug application or supplement of the failure of the owner of the Type II active pharmaceutical ingredient drug master file to pay the applicable fee.

children.label: ii
children.text: If such fee is not paid within 20 calendar days of the Secretary providing the notification, the abbreviated new drug application or supplement to an abbreviated new drug application shall not be received within the meaning of .section 355(j)(5)(A) of this title

children.heading: Abbreviated new drug application fee
children.label: 3 Abbreviated new drug application fee
children.text: Failure to pay a fee under subparagraph (A) or (F) of subsection (a)(3) within 20 calendar days of the applicable due date under subparagraph (C) of such subsection shall result in the abbreviated new drug application or the prior approval supplement to an abbreviated new drug application not being received within the meaning of until such outstanding fee is paid.section 355(j)(5)(A) of this title

children.heading: Generic drug facility fee and active pharmaceutical ingredient facility fee
children.label: 4 Generic drug facility fee and active pharmaceutical ingredient facility fee
i
children.text: The Secretary shall place the facility on a publicly available arrears list, such that no new abbreviated new drug application or supplement submitted on or after , from the person that is responsible for paying such fee, or any affiliate of that person, will be received within the meaning of .October 1, 2012section 355(j)(5)(A) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: Any new generic drug submission submitted on or after , that references such a facility shall not be received, within the meaning of if the outstanding facility fee is not paid within 20 calendar days of the Secretary providing the notification to the sponsor of the failure of the owner of the facility to pay the facility fee under subsection (a)(4)(C).October 1, 2012section 355(j)(5)(A) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: All drugs or active pharmaceutical ingredients manufactured in such a facility or containing an ingredient manufactured in such a facility shall be deemed misbranded under .section 352(aa) of this title

children.heading: Application of penalties
children.label: B Application of penalties
children.text: The penalties under this paragraph shall apply until the fee established by subsection (a)(4) is paid or the facility is removed from all generic drug submissions that refer to the facility.

children.heading: Notice
children.label: i Notice
children.text: If an abbreviated new drug application or supplement to an abbreviated new drug application submitted on or after , references a facility for which a facility fee has not been paid by the applicable date under subsection (a)(4)(C), the Secretary shall notify the sponsor of the generic drug submission of the failure of the owner of the facility to pay the facility fee.October 1, 2012

children.heading: Nonreceival
children.label: ii Nonreceival
children.text: If the facility fee is not paid within 20 calendar days of the Secretary providing the notification under clause (i), the abbreviated new drug application or supplement to an abbreviated new drug application shall not be received within the meaning of .section 355(j)(5)(A) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: The Secretary shall place the person on a publicly available arrears list.

children.label: ii
children.text: Any abbreviated new drug application submitted by the generic drug applicant or an affiliate of such applicant shall not be received, within the meaning of .section 355(j)(5)(A) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: All drugs marketed pursuant to any abbreviated new drug application held by such applicant or an affiliate of such applicant shall be deemed misbranded under .section 352(aa) of this title

children.heading: Application of penalties
children.label: B Application of penalties
children.text: The penalties under subparagraph (A) shall apply until the fee required under subsection (a)(5) is paid.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Fees under subsection (a) shall be refunded for a fiscal year beginning after fiscal year 2012, unless appropriations for salaries and expenses of the Food and Drug Administration for such fiscal year (excluding the amount of fees appropriated for such fiscal year) are equal to or greater than the amount of appropriations for the salaries and expenses of the Food and Drug Administration for fiscal year 2009 (excluding the amount of fees appropriated for such fiscal year) multiplied by the adjustment factor (as defined in ) applicable to the fiscal year involved.section 379j–41 of this title

children.heading: Authority
children.label: 2 Authority
children.text: If the Secretary does not assess fees under subsection (a) during any portion of a fiscal year and if at a later date in such fiscal year the Secretary may assess such fees, the Secretary may assess and collect such fees, without any modification in the rate, at any time in such fiscal year notwithstanding the provisions of subsection (a) relating to the date fees are to be paid.

children.heading: Crediting and availability of fees
children.label: i Crediting and availability of fees

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Fees authorized under subsection (a) shall be collected and available for obligation only to the extent and in the amount provided in advance in appropriations Acts, subject to paragraph (2). Such fees are authorized to remain available until expended. Such sums as may be necessary may be transferred from the Food and Drug Administration salaries and expenses appropriation account without fiscal year limitation to such appropriation account for salaries and expenses with such fiscal year limitation. The sums transferred shall be available solely for human generic drug activities.

children.label: i
children.text: subject to subparagraph (C), shall be collected and available in each fiscal year in an amount not to exceed the amount specified in appropriation Acts, or otherwise made available for obligation for such fiscal year; and

children.label: I
children.text: for fiscal year 2023, to defray the costs of human generic drug activities (including such costs for an additional number of full-time equivalent positions in the Department of Health and Human Services to be engaged in such activities), only if the Secretary allocates for such purpose an amount for such fiscal year (excluding amounts from fees collected under this section) no less than $97,000,000 multiplied by the adjustment factor defined in applicable to such fiscal year; andsection 379j–41(3) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: for fiscal year 2024 and each subsequent fiscal year, to defray the costs of human generic drug activities (including such costs for an additional number of full-time equivalent positions in the Department of Health and Human Services to be engaged in such activities), only if the sum of the amounts allocated by the Secretary for such costs, excluding costs paid from fees collected under this section, plus other costs for the maintenance, renovation, and repair of facilities and acquisition, maintenance, and repair of fixtures, furniture, and other necessary materials and supplies in connection with human generic drug activities, is no less than $97,000,000 multiplied by the adjustment factor defined in applicable to the fiscal year involved.section 379j–41(3) of this title

children.heading: Compliance
children.label: B Compliance
children.text: The Secretary shall be considered to have met the requirements of subparagraph (A)(ii) in any fiscal year if the costs funded by appropriations and allocated as described in subclause (I) or (II) of such subparagraph, as applicable, are not more than 10 percent below the level specified in such subparagraph.

children.heading: Provision for early payments
children.label: C Provision for early payments
children.text: Payment of fees authorized under this section for a fiscal year, prior to the due date for such fees, may be accepted by the Secretary in accordance with authority provided in advance in a prior year appropriations Act.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 3 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: For each of the fiscal years 2023 through 2027, there is authorized to be appropriated for fees under this section an amount equivalent to the total revenue amount determined under subsection (b) for the fiscal year, as adjusted under subsection (c), if applicable, or as otherwise affected under paragraph (2) of this subsection.

children.heading: Collection of unpaid fees
children.label: j Collection of unpaid fees
children.text: In any case where the Secretary does not receive payment of a fee assessed under subsection (a) within 30 calendar days after it is due, such fee shall be treated as a claim of the United States Government subject to subchapter II of chapter 37 of title 31.

children.heading: Construction
children.label: k Construction
children.text: This section may not be construed to require that the number of full-time equivalent positions in the Department of Health and Human Services, for officers, employees, and advisory committees not engaged in human generic drug activities, be reduced to offset the number of officers, employees, and advisory committees so engaged.

children.heading: Exemption from fees
children.label: 1 Exemption from fees
children.text: Submission of an application for a positron emission tomography drug or active pharmaceutical ingredient for a positron emission tomography drug shall not require the payment of any fee under this section. Facilities that solely produce positron emission tomography drugs shall not be required to pay a facility fee as established in subsection (a)(4).

children.heading: Identification requirement
children.label: 2 Identification requirement
children.text: Facilities that produce positron emission tomography drugs or active pharmaceutical ingredients of such drugs are required to be identified pursuant to subsection (f).

children.heading: Disputes concerning fees
children.label: m Disputes concerning fees
children.text: To qualify for the return of a fee claimed to have been paid in error under this section, a person shall submit to the Secretary a written request justifying such return within 180 calendar days after such fee was paid.

children.heading: Substantially complete applications
children.label: n Substantially complete applications
children.text: An abbreviated new drug application that is not considered to be received within the meaning of because of failure to pay an applicable fee under this provision within the time period specified in subsection (g) shall be deemed not to have been “substantially complete” on the date of its submission within the meaning of . An abbreviated new drug application that is not substantially complete on the date of its submission solely because of failure to pay an applicable fee under the preceding sentence shall be deemed substantially complete and received within the meaning of as of the date such applicable fee is received.section 355(j)(5)(A) of this titlesection 355(j)(5)(B)(iv)(II)(cc) of this titlesection 355(j)(5)(A) of this title

children.heading: Information on abbreviated new drug applications owned by applicants and their affiliates
children.label: o Information on abbreviated new drug applications owned by applicants and their affiliates
A
children.text: all approved abbreviated new drug applications owned by such person; and

children.label: B
children.text: if any affiliate of such person also owns an abbreviated new drug application, all affiliates that own any such abbreviated new drug application and all approved abbreviated new drug applications owned by any such affiliate.

children.heading: Format and method
children.label: 2 Format and method
children.text: The Secretary shall specify in guidance the format and method for submission of lists under this subsection.
